behavior_summary: U8P_VARDION::cast: Marks Vardion as enraged (sets vardionMad) and delegates to the spell-combat handler SPELCMBT::combatSpellLoop so the NPC reacts to being targeted by a spell.

U8P_VARDION::AvatarStoleSomething: Implements a staged anti-theft response. If the avatar is not authorized (checks vardionJoined, sponsorTest, keyOfTheAcolyte) Vardion issues escalating verbal warnings (increments vardionWarned) and, on final escalation or special-match condition, freezes the avatar (setAvatarInStasis), barks an attack line, sets the avatar as the NPC target (Npc::setTarget) and transitions to combat.
U8P_VARDION::look: Returns the 'U8P_VARDION::look' text shown when the player examines Vardion; selects among name/title/deceased variants based on vardionName, vardionJob and Npc::isDead(this).

U8P_VARDION::enterFastArea / U8P_VARDION::leaveFastArea: U8P_VARDION::enterFastArea is an area-entry trigger that updates item frames, performs conditional item cleanup/destruction (when acolyteKilled == 0x02), optionally teleports Vardion to an inner sanctum (goToInnerSanctum), and may set the avatar as target or initiate movement (MOVE::func0080) depending on flags; U8P_VARDION::leaveFastArea clears the associated reference (FREE::resetRef).

U8P_VARDION::use and U8P_VARDION::func073C/U8P_VARDION::func54C4: 'U8P_VARDION::use' begins/ends conversations and routes to specialized branches — angry behavior, allied behavior, daemon-scene branches, or the main dialogue menu. U8P_VARDION::func073C is the primary menu/dialogue loop building dynamic menus (local3) and routing player choices (local2/local4) to scene helpers (VARDION2::*). U8P_VARDION::func54C4 is a yes/no monologue to request First Acolyte status and sets vardionGroveled to prevent repetition.
notable_control_flow: U8P_VARDION::cast() is a short reactive process: write vardionMad then call SPELCMBT::combatSpellLoop and suspend — control is handed immediately to external combat logic (high confidence).

U8P_VARDION::AvatarStoleSomething() performs an access check (vardionJoined, sponsorTest, keyOfTheAcolyte) and only proceeds to warnings/attack when the player lacks privileges; warnings are tracked via vardionWarned and escalate to lethal action (medium-high confidence).

Dialogue loop in U8P_VARDION::func073C builds a menu (local3), appends a 'Farewell.' exit repeatedly (FREE::moveToEndOfList), presents it with Ask and suspend, and dispatches on the captured choice via strcmp on local2/local4; vardionEnclave is set the first time the enclave exposition is given (high confidence for high-level structure, medium for some menu details).

U8P_VARDION::enterFastArea() contains two distinct behaviors: world/item cleanup (acolyteKilled branch) and actor interaction routing (teleport, movement, or setting target) based on flags like goToInnerSanctum and avatarMasterSorcerer (medium confidence).
open_questions: U8P_VARDION::cast(param1) and U8P_VARDION::AvatarStoleSomething(param1): What exact values/encodings param1 carries (spell id, caster id, item handle)? The code treats it generically (low-medium confidence on role).
Some METHOD::func* and VARDION2::* calls are external helpers; their precise in-game effects (timing, animations, quest progression) are inferred but not certain (medium confidence).
Precise semantics of some local temporaries in U8P_VARDION::func073C (local5..local11) are unclear — they appear to track dialogue sub-state but exact meanings are low-confidence.

In U8P_VARDION::enterFastArea(), the positional comparison logic deciding which items to destroy uses Item::getX values; the exact spatial rule and which items are targets is inferred from structure but not fully certain (medium-low confidence).

main_function.name: U8P_VARDION::cast
main_function.purpose: React to a spell/U8P_VARDION::cast affecting this NPC by flagging Vardion as enraged and handing control to the combat-spell loop for reactive behavior.
entry_conditions: Invoked when a spell/U8P_VARDION::cast process targets or affects this NPC (process type 0x021D).
Receives one uword parameter (param1) that is likely context about the U8P_VARDION::cast (spell id or caster context).

function_map.name: U8P_VARDION::cast
function_map.role: Reactive spell-target handler that sets vardionMad and calls SPELCMBT::combatSpellLoop.

function_map.name: U8P_VARDION::AvatarStoleSomething
function_map.role: Theft/anti-theft response: access checks, escalating warnings (vardionWarned), and transition to combat via setAvatarInStasis and Npc::setTarget.

function_map.name: U8P_VARDION::look
function_map.role: Look/inspect result provider; chooses name/job/dead variants for display.

function_map.name: U8P_VARDION::schedule
function_map.role: Periodic scheduler that prepares deferred actions via METHOD::func143A and acts on completion via METHOD::func133F.

function_map.name: U8P_VARDION::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area entry trigger: item frame updates, conditional item cleanup/destruction, optional teleport to inner sanctum, and movement/targeting initiation.

function_map.name: U8P_VARDION::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area exit cleanup: resets the fast-area reference (FREE::resetRef).

function_map.name: U8P_VARDION::use
function_map.role: Handle player 'U8P_VARDION::use' interactions: open/close gump, start conversation, and route to dialogue/scene/movement helpers based on flags.

function_map.name: U8P_VARDION::func073C
function_map.role: Primary dialogue/menu routine: assemble dynamic menu (local3), Ask/suspend to capture choices (local2/local4), and dispatch to scene helpers (VARDION2::*) or set flags like vardionEnclave.

function_map.name: U8P_VARDION::func54C4
function_map.role: Monologue for acolyte promotion request: yes/no prompt (FREE::askYesNo), set vardionGroveled to avoid repeat, and follow-up bookkeeping via METHOD::func15E7.

internal_calls.purpose: Delegate actual spell-combat reaction loop after marking Vardion as enraged.
Follow-up cleanup or state advance after theft-warning/combat transition (inferred).
Transfer control to main dialogue/menu when no special-case flags apply.
Invoke the acolyte-request monologue when that branch is selected (dead-case or special-case branch).

external_calls.purpose: External combat spell handler that processes NPC reaction to an incoming spell (high confidence).
Clear or reset an internal reference before issuing warnings/combat (low-medium confidence).
Bookkeeping/alert helper invoked as part of theft response preparation (inferred).
Temporarily freeze/unfreeze the avatar while delivering threatening barks and preparing attack (high confidence).
Set Vardion's combat target to the avatar to initiate attack (high confidence).
Change item appearance/frame as part of area-entry visual or state update (medium confidence).
Remove world items during cleanup when acolyteKilled == 0x02 (medium confidence).
Teleport Vardion to inner sanctum when goToInnerSanctum is set (medium-high confidence).
Initiate NPC movement toward a player or destination when access conditions permit (medium confidence).
Open the NPC interaction UI when the player uses Vardion (dead-case toggling and normal 'U8P_VARDION::use').
Start and finish dialog sessions for player interaction flows (high confidence).
Append the fixed 'Farewell.' menu option to the dynamic menu list local3 across loop iterations (high confidence).
Execute a scene or behavior tied to a particular dialogue choice (inferred).
Execute another scene/behavior tied to a different dialogue branch (inferred).
Present a yes/no prompt and capture the result for the acolyte promotion request (high confidence).
Routine called at entry/exit of the monologue for setup/teardown or bookkeeping (medium confidence).

reads.name: vardionJoined
reads.likely_effect: Used to treat the avatar as an ally/authorized actor and skip theft punishment or route allied dialogue.

reads.name: sponsorTest
reads.likely_effect: Part of access/ally checks for theft and fast-area movement initiation (helps decide if actions like MOVE::func0080 should occur).

reads.name: keyOfTheAcolyte
reads.likely_effect: Access flag affecting theft handling and a 'key' branch in U8P_VARDION::use() that triggers a daemon/scene (DAEMSCEN::func4355).

reads.name: acolyteKilled
reads.likely_effect: When equal to 0x02 enables item cleanup/destruction logic in U8P_VARDION::enterFastArea and routes to an associated daemon scene in U8P_VARDION::use().

reads.name: inDaemonScene
reads.likely_effect: Prevents scheduler movement/behavior and affects dialogue/fast-area decisions related to scene contexts.

reads.name: goToInnerSanctum
reads.likely_effect: Triggers teleportation of Vardion to an inner sanctum when set during fast-area entry.

reads.name: vardionName
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether 'Who are you?' or name variants appear in U8P_VARDION::look() / dialogue menus.

reads.name: vardionJob
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether 'What do you do?' or job/title variants appear in U8P_VARDION::look() / dialogue menus.

reads.name: avatarMasterSorcerer
reads.likely_effect: If true, may cause Vardion to target the avatar on fast-area entry (special privileged player status).

reads.name: vardionOffer
reads.likely_effect: Influences initial dialogue menu setup (choice of menu variants) in U8P_VARDION::func073C.

reads.name: vardionGroveled
reads.likely_effect: Read to determine whether to show the full acolyte-request monologue; prevents repeat offering when already set.

writes.name: vardionMad
writes.likely_effect: Set by U8P_VARDION::cast() to mark Vardion as enraged/aggressive; influences U8P_VARDION::use()-branch and triggers angry behavior routines.

writes.name: vardionWarned
writes.likely_effect: Incremented by U8P_VARDION::AvatarStoleSomething to track warning progression before lethal action; used to select warning texts and final escalation.

writes.name: vardionEnclave
writes.likely_effect: Set by U8P_VARDION::func073C when the player first asks about the enclave to avoid repeating exposition; marks knowledge as given.

writes.name: vardionGroveled
writes.likely_effect: Set by U8P_VARDION::func54C4 after offering the acolyte request so the monologue is not repeated; records that the request has been presented.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_VARDION::cast: Marks Vardion as enraged (sets vardionMad) and delegates to the spell-combat handler SPELCMBT::combatSpellLoop so the NPC reacts to being targeted by a spell.
